title: Sodium does not cause high blood pressure

chunks.title: Sodium and Hypertension
chunks.summary: The speaker explains that sodium does not cause hypertension in healthy, insulin-sensitive individuals. It discusses temporary increases in blood volume due to sodium intake and the body's ability to regulate this through the kidneys.
chunks.transcript: but Paul doesn't salt cause hypertension no in a healthy individual who is insulin sensitive eating sodium does not cause high blood pressure though my body would Rebel and tell me not to do this I could eat 20 plus grams of sodium per day and not develop chronic hypertension would I have an increased blood volume for a day before my kidneys did something called naturalis and got rid of that sodium yeah my blood volume might be slightly high I might actually have temporary hypertension if I ate a massive amount of sodium but my body is going to tell me to get rid of it it's not going to be pleasant to do that and the kidneys will adjust the kidneys will get rid of that excess sodium and my blood pressure will return to normal so

chunks.title: Insulin Resistance and Sodium Retention
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to insulin resistance, highlighting how it leads to excessive sodium retention in the kidneys. It emphasizes the dangers of low sodium diets for insulin-resistant individuals, including potential health issues such as orthostatic hypotension and erectile dysfunction.
chunks.transcript: have insulin resistance there will be excess resorption excess holding on to sodium at the level of the kidney so what can we do if we don't correct the root cause of hypertension which is insulin resistance we must get rid of the sodium the problem is that sodium is critical for so many functions in the human body we know that in individuals who are insulin resistant who limit their sodium they will have more orthostatic hypotension meaning they get up they get lightheaded and they can fall over or black out they will have more erectile dysfunction they will have lower blood volume lower energy low sodium diets are a nightmare for humans it is no way to live as a human to be a low sodium individual limiting your sodium too much will cause you to feel miserable cause all of the issues I've talked about orthostatic hypotension potentially erectile dysfunction and you will activate your renin andriot tensin

chunks.title: Recommended Sodium Intake
chunks.summary: The speaker shares personal experiences regarding daily sodium intake, advocating for a balanced approach to sodium consumption. They mention the need for higher sodium in ketogenic dieters due to insulin's role in managing electrolyte levels.
chunks.transcript: that system is primed to do is to conserve sodium if you don't get enough so the renin aldosterone ratio is a critical thing you can look at in humans if you limit your sodium too much you will see aldosterone and and Rise because your body is trying to conserve the sodium what is an ideal amount of sodium for humans see what works for you I would say that on a daily basis I probably have six to8 grams of salt in my diet I use a low microplastic sea salt I'm very intentional about that

chunks.title: Effects of Insulin on Electrolyte Balance
chunks.summary: The video continues by discussing how insulin affects sodium retention and the subsequent balance of other electrolytes. It argues against the fear of carbohydrates, suggesting that moderate carbohydrate consumption can aid in better electrolyte management.
chunks.transcript: half or four grams of sodium per day sodium chloride is of course a salt so half of the weight of the sodium chloride will be the sodium that's what works for me ketogenic dieters often need more than that because remember that the actions of insulin at the level of the kidney are critical for the maintenance of proper electrolyte levels I did a podcast previously where I talked about why I no longer think a ketogenic diet is ideal for humans why I stopped a ketogenic diet in the past and one of the things I talked about in that podcast was that a postprandial after eating spike in your insulin is a healthy good thing it affects hormones positively it allows you to retain the sodium that you eat so your electrolytes can be balanced when sodium becomes disordered all of the electrolytes become disordered chloride potassium magnesium it's a nightmare people on ketogenic diets often must Chase electrolytes round and round like a dog chasing its tail don't fear carbohydrates I believe that fruit is the least toxic source of carbohydrates but getting some carbohydrates in your meat-based diet will improve your electrolyte maintenance and conversely excess insulin in a state of hyperinsulinemia will lead to excess

chunks.title: Hyperinsulinemia and Health Consequences
chunks.summary: The speaker outlines the negative effects of hyperinsulinemia, including increased sympathetic tone and lipid profile changes. They touch on how this condition contributes to hypertension and can impair nitric oxide production.
chunks.transcript: insulin do it causes increased sympathetic Tone It causes that fight ORF flight nervous system to go up it increases the production of andot tensin to and it appears and I'll speak about this at the end of the podcast that in hypertensive individuals angot 2 may be increased independent of insulin as well hyperinsulinemia decreases the formation of nitric oxide through many of the second messenger Cascades hyperinsulinemia also appears to raise LDL lower HDL and raise triglycerides oh now we're back to lipids yeah it's all
